sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Terminology 37:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Terminology 37:1
items.html: When the screen is refined from its coarseness and the coupling stops from it, the light departs from the vessel, and the letters are regarded as being without filling. This is because the vessels are the letters, and the filling is the measure of the coarseness in the screen. Since it was refined from the coarseness, it lacks the filling. (Item 58)
